theory.hypothesis: Reina can spell most silent-letter words with scaffolded letter-by-letter support, and can recognize most high-frequency words fluently. The silent-letter group requires spelling-production evidence because the teacher heading explicitly names a pattern-based skill; the high-frequency group requires recognition and fluency evidence because the heading signals sight-word goals, not spelling production.
evidenceSummary: No recent canonical activity evidence exists for Reina on this homework.
Teacher headings distinguish 'Silent Letters' (spelling pattern) from 'High-Frequency Words' (recognition/fluency).
Benchmark Advance Unit 9 Week 3 scope confirms silent-letter patterns are spelling-construction targets and high-frequency words are reading-fluency targets.
Reina is grade 2; stamina and motivation are appropriate for two required baseline activities before choice.
theory.intervention: Measure silent-letter spelling production with Word Radar partial_visual_recall (letter-by-letter input, hidden word during response, captured response required) to establish which words Reina can construct independently versus which need targeted support. Measure high-frequency recognition with Pronunciation visible_read (whole-word speak, visible word, captured response required) to establish which words Reina can read fluently versus which need pronunciation or decoding support. After both baseline nodes, offer Mystery choice lab for preference evidence on reward wrappers. Lock Quest and Boss until baseline and Mystery evidence clarify the theory.
supportCriteria: Word Radar silent-letter baseline shows 8+ first-try correct with low latency, supporting strong pattern awareness.
Pronunciation high-frequency baseline shows 9+ fluent first-pass reads, supporting strong recognition.
Mystery choice completed with positive engagement, supporting motivation for continued work.
reviseCriteria: Word Radar silent-letter baseline shows 4–7 correct or multiple retries, indicating partial pattern knowledge and need for targeted support on exact missed words.
Pronunciation high-frequency baseline shows 5–8 fluent reads or hesitation on 2–5 words, indicating those exact words need pronunciation or visible-read support.
Mystery choice skipped or abandoned, indicating wrapper mismatch or stamina limit.
falsifyCriteria: Word Radar silent-letter baseline shows 0–3 correct or pervasive confusion, falsifying pattern-awareness hypothesis and indicating need for explicit teaching.
Pronunciation high-frequency baseline shows hesitation or misread on 6+ words, falsifying recognition hypothesis and indicating need for explicit decoding.
Both baseline activities abandoned or frustrated, falsifying engagement hypothesis and indicating need for relationship repair or stamina check.

plannedMeasurements.target: silent_letters group: sign, know, write, thumb, comb, gnat, knock, knife, wrong, climb
plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: baseline spelling construction with partial visual recall
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: First-try correct on 8+ words with low latency and no retry supports that silent-letter pattern awareness is strong
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 4–7 correct on first try, or multiple retries, suggests partial pattern knowledge and indicates need for targeted support on exact missed words
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: 0–3 correct, or pervasive confusion across all silent-letter types, falsifies pattern-awareness hypothesis and indicates need for explicit teaching before practice

plannedMeasurements.target: high_frequency_words group: among, building, circle, decided, finally, heavy, include, nothing, special, wheel
plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: baseline read-aloud fluency with visible word
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Fluent first-pass pronunciation on 9+ words with no hesitation or support requests supports that recognition is strong and only light spaced checks are needed
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 5–8 fluent reads, or hesitation/skip on 2–5 words, suggests partial recognition and indicates those exact words need pronunciation or visible-read support
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Hesitation or misread on 6+ words, or pervasive decoding struggle, falsifies recognition hypothesis and indicates need for explicit decoding or word-building before fluency practice

plannedMeasurements.target: child preference between puzzle-style and action-style reward wrappers
plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: preference evidence from choice, completion, and replay
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Chosen option completed with replay or positive affect supports preference for that wrapper type
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Chosen option abandoned or frustrated indicates wrapper mismatch; skipped option is weak neutral
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Both options skipped or session abandoned falsifies engagement hypothesis and indicates need for relationship repair or stamina check
